characters.name: Ishmael
characters.description: The narrator and protagonist, a man who turns to seafaring as a sailor when life becomes oppressive. He has worked as both a schoolmaster and merchant sailor before deciding to embark on a whaling voyage.

characters.name: Cato
characters.description: A historical/philosophical figure referenced by Ishmael as someone who threw himself upon his sword, contrasted with Ishmael's preference for taking to ships.

characters.name: Gabriel
characters.description: The archangel referenced by Ishmael in his philosophical musings about servitude and dignity.

characters.name: Narcissus
characters.description: A mythological figure who drowned trying to grasp his reflection in water, used by Ishmael as a metaphor for humanity's relationship with the ocean.

timeline.event: Ishmael decides to go to sea due to his melancholy state
Ishmael reflects on his past experiences as a merchant sailor
Ishmael decides to embark on a whaling voyage

settings.name: Manhattan
settings.description: The insular city of the Manhattoes, surrounded by wharves and commerce, with its extreme downtown at the Battery where crowds of water-gazers gather. Streets lead waterward, and thousands of men stand in ocean reveries along the waterfront.

settings.name: Corlears Hook
settings.description: A waterfront location in Manhattan mentioned as part of a walking route around the city.

settings.name: Coenties Slip
settings.description: A waterfront location in Manhattan, part of the route where water-gazers congregate.

settings.name: Whitehall
settings.description: A location in Manhattan mentioned in the walking route around the waterfront.

settings.name: The Battery
settings.description: Manhattan's extreme downtown area where a noble mole is washed by waves and cooled by ocean breezes.

settings.name: Valley of the Saco
settings.description: A romantic landscape with trees, meadows, cattle, cottages with sleepy smoke, and distant woodlands winding toward mountains, all centered around a magic stream.

settings.name: Rockaway Beach
settings.description: A beach destination mentioned in reference to a poor Tennessee poet's travel dilemma.

world_rules: Passengers must pay to travel by ship, while sailors are paid for their work
Ship hierarchy exists with Commodores and Captains at the top, sailors at the bottom
All men are essentially slaves in some physical or metaphysical sense
The Fates act as invisible police officers, secretly influencing human actions
Providence has a grand programme that determines individual destinies

voice_profile.style_notes: Dense, meandering prose with frequent digressions, rhetorical questions, and literary/mythological allusions. Uses long, complex sentences interspersed with shorter declarative statements. Heavy use of metaphor and philosophical reflection.

plot_threads.thread: Why Ishmael has chosen to embark specifically on a whaling voyage rather than another merchant sailing job
Ishmael's fascination with the great whale and the mysterious perils associated with whaling
The identity and significance of the 'grand hooded phantom, like a snow hill in the air' that appears in Ishmael's visions
